title: Nageia mannii
taxonomy: Nageia mannii is a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae. This family is a part of the order Podocarpales, which is a group of coniferous plants that are primarily found in the Southern Hemisphere. The Podocarpaceae family is one of the oldest living conifer families, with fossil records dating back to the Cretaceous period. The species Nageia mannii is endemic to the island of Borneo and is often found in humid, tropical forests. It is a relatively rare species that is threatened by habitat loss and degradation.
uses: Nageia mannii has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties, including treating various health issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing pain and combating infections. In traditional medicine, extracts from Nageia mannii are used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and seeds are often used in teas and infusions to promote digestion and relieve digestive discomfort. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Nageia mannii's medicinal uses and to establish safe dosages for human consumption.

These active constituents are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
